tasks.title: Cursor-based pagination, fix mutation return types, and auto-generated API docs
tasks.description:
Three related API quality improvements: fix mutation return types, add cursor-based pagination, and generate API documentation.

**1. Fix inconsistent mutation return types:**
- `deleteComment` (resolvers/task.ts line 389-399, typedef in comment.ts line 37): Currently returns `Boolean!`. Change to return the deleted `Comment` object â€” fetch the comment before deleting, delete it, then return the fetched object. Update typedef: `deleteComment(commentId: ID!): Comment!`.
- `markAllNotificationsRead` (resolvers/notification.ts line 60-67, typedef in notification.ts line 28): Currently returns `Boolean!`. Change to return `Int!` representing the count of notifications marked read (use the `count` from `updateMany` result). Update typedef: `markAllNotificationsRead: Int!`.

**2. Add cursor-based pagination to `activities` and `reports` queries:**
- `activities` query (resolvers/task.ts lines 61-77, typedef in comment.ts line 31): Currently `activities(projectId: ID, taskId: ID, limit: Int): [Activity!]!` with only `take`. Add cursor-based pagination:
  - New typedef: `activities(projectId: ID, taskId: ID, limit: Int, cursor: String): ActivityConnection!`
  - New type: `type ActivityConnection { activities: [Activity!]!, hasMore: Boolean!, nextCursor: String }`
  - Resolver: use Prisma cursor pagination (`cursor: { activityId: cursor }, skip: 1` when cursor provided). Set `hasMore` by fetching `limit + 1` and checking length. `nextCursor` is the last activity's `activityId`.
- `reports` query (resolvers/ai.ts lines 837-851, typedef in report.ts line 49): Same pattern. `reports(projectId: ID!, type: String, limit: Int, cursor: String): ReportConnection!` with `type ReportConnection { reports: [Report!]!, hasMore: Boolean!, nextCursor: String }`.
- Follow the existing `TaskConnection` pattern (typedefs/task.ts line 95) as a reference.

**3. Auto-generated API documentation:**
- Install `spectaql` or use a simpler approach: create a route at `GET /api/docs` that serves a static HTML page with the full GraphQL schema SDL and operation documentation.
- Simplest approach: add a `GET /api/docs` route in a new `apps/api/src/routes/docs.ts` that:
  - Uses `printSchema` from `graphql` to get the SDL from the built schema
  - Renders a simple HTML page with the schema, organized by type (queries, mutations, types)
  - Include auth instructions (Bearer token required for all except signup/login)
  - Include example requests using fetch
- Register the route in `app.ts`.
- Alternative: use `graphql-yoga`'s built-in GraphiQL (already available in dev) and just enable it in production by configuring the yoga options. Check if `graphiql: true` can be set in production mode in `schema.ts`.

**Important:** After modifying any Prisma-related queries, ensure you DON'T need a migration â€” these changes are resolver/typedef only. Run `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build` to validate.

Acceptance criteria:
- `deleteComment` returns the deleted `Comment` object
- `markAllNotificationsRead` returns the count of updated notifications
- `activities` and `reports` queries support cursor-based pagination with `hasMore` and `nextCursor`
- `GET /api/docs` serves API documentation (or GraphiQL is enabled in production)
- `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build` all pass

tasks.title: Custom fields on tasks + saved filters/views + JSON column migration
tasks.description:
Add custom fields, saved filter views, and migrate JSON string columns to proper types. Run `npx prisma migrate dev` after schema changes.

**1. Custom fields on tasks** â€” Full vertical slice:
- **Prisma models** (task.prisma): Add `CustomField` model with fields: `id`, `orgId`, `projectId`, `name`, `fieldType` (enum: TEXT, NUMBER, DATE, DROPDOWN), `options` (String? â€” JSON array for dropdown options), `required` (Boolean, default false), `position` (Int). Add `CustomFieldValue` model: `id`, `customFieldId`, `taskId`, `value` (String â€” stores all types as strings, parsed by type). Add relations to Task and Project.
- **GraphQL types** (typedefs/task.ts): Add `CustomField { id, name, fieldType, options, required, position }` and `CustomFieldValue { id, field: CustomField!, value: String }` types. Add `Task.customFieldValues: [CustomFieldValue!]!` field. Add mutations: `createCustomField(projectId, name, fieldType, options?, required?)`, `updateCustomField(id, name?, options?, required?)`, `deleteCustomField(id)`, `setCustomFieldValue(taskId, customFieldId, value)`. Add query: `customFields(projectId): [CustomField!]!`.
- **Resolvers** (resolvers/task.ts): Implement CRUD. Field resolver on Task for `customFieldValues` using a DataLoader or direct query. `setCustomFieldValue` should upsert (create or update).
- **Frontend** â€” Create `TaskCustomFieldsSection.tsx` in `taskdetail/`: Render each custom field with appropriate input (text input, number input, date picker, dropdown select). Add to TaskDetailPanel after the existing fields panel.
- **Project settings** â€” Add a "Custom Fields" section in `ProjectSettingsModal.tsx` where admins can create/edit/delete custom fields for the project, with drag-to-reorder.
- **FilterBar integration** â€” Add custom field filters to FilterBar. For each custom field, add a filter control (text search for TEXT, range for NUMBER, date picker for DATE, dropdown for DROPDOWN). Update `useTaskFiltering.ts` to filter by custom field values.

**2. Saved filters / views:**
- **Prisma model** (project.prisma): Add `SavedFilter { id, projectId, userId, name, filters (String â€” JSON), isDefault (Boolean, default false), createdAt }`. Add relation to Project.
- **GraphQL** (typedefs/project.ts): Type + `savedFilters(projectId)` query + `saveFilter(projectId, name, filters)`, `updateFilter(id, name?, filters?)`, `deleteFilter(id)` mutations.
- **FilterBar UI**: Add a "Save current filter" button and a dropdown to load saved filters. Show saved filter names as pills/tabs above the filter bar.

**3. JSON string columns â†’ proper types:**
- `Sprint.columns`: Change from `String` with JSON default to proper handling. Since Prisma doesn't have a native `String[]` for all DBs, keep as `String` but add a helper function `parseColumns(sprint)` and `serializeColumns(columns)` to centralize the JSON.parse/stringify calls scattered across resolvers. Remove raw `JSON.parse` calls from resolvers and hooks.
- `Task.suggestedTools`: Same pattern â€” add `parseSuggestedTools(task)` helper.
- This is NOT a migration â€” just code cleanup to centralize JSON parsing.

Acceptance criteria:
- Custom fields can be created per project with 4 field types (text, number, date, dropdown)
- Custom field values render in TaskDetailPanel with appropriate inputs
- Custom fields appear as filter options in FilterBar
- Saved filters can be created, loaded, and deleted
- JSON string column parsing is centralized in helper functions
- Prisma migration runs successfully
- `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build` all pass
tasks.reviewNotes: Typecheck fails: Prisma client does not know about CustomField, CustomFieldValue, or SavedFilter models. You need to run prisma generate (cd apps/api && npx prisma generate) after adding schema models so the Prisma client types are regenerated. All 17 errors are Property X does not exist on type PrismaClient. After running prisma migrate dev, run pnpm typecheck to verify, then mark completed again.

tasks.title: Task templates, file attachments, and recurring tasks
tasks.description:
Add task templates, file attachments, and recurring tasks. This task depends on task-002 (same branch, same files). Run `npx prisma migrate dev` after schema changes. Rebase on main after task-002 is merged before starting this task.

**1. Task templates** â€” Full vertical slice:
- **Prisma model** (project.prisma): `TaskTemplate { id, orgId, projectId, name, title, description, status, priority, taskType, estimatedHours, storyPoints, labels (String â€” JSON array of label names), customFieldDefaults (String â€” JSON), createdAt }`. Add relation to Project.
- **GraphQL** (typedefs/project.ts): Type + `taskTemplates(projectId)` query + `createTaskTemplate(projectId, name, title, description?, ...)`, `updateTaskTemplate(id, ...)`, `deleteTaskTemplate(id)`, `createTaskFromTemplate(templateId, projectId)` mutations.
- **Resolvers** (resolvers/project.ts): `createTaskFromTemplate` fetches the template, creates a task with all template fields pre-filled, applies label associations, returns the created task.
- **Frontend** â€” `TaskTemplateModal.tsx`: Modal listing existing templates with "Use" and "Delete" buttons. "Create template" form pre-filled from current task (if opened from task detail) or blank. Add "Templates" button in ProjectDetail toolbar (near the Add Task button, around line 336).

**2. File attachments** â€” Full vertical slice:
- **Prisma model** (task.prisma): `Attachment { id, orgId, taskId, filename, originalName, mimeType, sizeBytes, uploadedBy, createdAt }`. Add relation to Task. Files stored on disk at `uploads/{orgId}/{id}-{filename}`.
- **Backend** â€” Install `multer` (`pnpm --filter api add multer @types/multer`). Create `apps/api/src/routes/upload.ts`:
  - `POST /api/upload` â€” multer middleware (10MB limit, validate mime types), requires auth (read JWT from Authorization header), creates Attachment record, returns attachment metadata. Store files in `uploads/` directory (create if missing).
  - `GET /api/files/:id` â€” serve file by attachment ID (validate org ownership), set Content-Disposition header.
  - `DELETE /api/files/:id` â€” delete file from disk + DB record.
- Register routes in `app.ts` (before the GraphQL handler).
- **GraphQL** (typedefs/task.ts): Add `Attachment { id, filename, originalName, mimeType, sizeBytes, createdAt }` type. Add `Task.attachments: [Attachment!]!` field resolver. Add `deleteAttachment(id: ID!): Boolean!` mutation (REST handles upload, GraphQL handles metadata queries + delete).
- **Frontend** â€” `TaskAttachmentsSection.tsx` in `taskdetail/`: File list with download links, delete buttons, drag-and-drop upload area (use native `<input type="file">` with drag events â€” no extra deps). Upload via `fetch('/api/upload', { method: 'POST', body: formData })`. Add to TaskDetailPanel after the description section.

**3. Recurring tasks:**
- **Prisma fields** (task.prisma): Add to Task model: `recurrenceRule String?` (cron expression or simple rule like "daily", "weekly", "monthly"), `recurrenceParentId String?` (FK to parent recurring task), `nextRecurrence DateTime?`.
- **GraphQL** (typedefs/task.ts): Add `recurrenceRule`, `recurrenceParentId`, `nextRecurrence` to Task type. Add to `updateTask` input.
- **Logic**: When a recurring task is marked `done`, check if it has a `recurrenceRule`. If so, create a new task with the same fields, reset status to `todo`, set `recurrenceParentId` to the original task, calculate `nextRecurrence` based on the rule. Implement this in the `updateTask` resolver when status changes to `done`.
- **Frontend** â€” Add a recurrence toggle/selector in `TaskFieldsPanel.tsx`: dropdown with options (None, Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Custom cron). Show "Recurring" badge on task cards.

Acceptance criteria:
- Task templates can be created, listed, and used to create new tasks
- Files can be uploaded (drag-and-drop or file picker), downloaded, and deleted on tasks
- Recurring tasks auto-create a new instance when marked done
- Prisma migration runs successfully
- `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build` all pass
